behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears at a module boundary: summary-write logic must query persisted per-dimension findings and enforce a cross-record constraint. A unit test of the formatter/validator alone can miss wiring bugs (wrong round key, stale read, missing join/filter).
behaviors.test_description: Set up a round in a local test DB with at least one dimension verdict = FAIL. Attempt to persist a summary marked PASS through the real summary-save service/repository path. Assert the write is rejected (or transaction rolls back) with a domain error, and no PASS-finalized state is stored for that round.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: This is specifically about data-source precedence across components (gate evaluator + storage + summary text). A unit test of gate logic with in-memory inputs can miss real miswiring where production code reads summary text instead of findings.
behaviors.test_description: Persist contradictory data for one round: summary text says all PASS, but stored per-dimension findings include FAIL. Run the real stop-gate computation path. Assert verdict is non-PASS (blocked/fail) and decision rationale references stored findings rather than summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Two coupled effects must both occur: authorization/flag check and durable audit emission. Real failures are often in handoff (flag parsed but not enforced, warning emitted but not persisted).
behaviors.test_description: Attempt manual summary override without override flag via the real API/service and assert rejection. Then repeat with override flag enabled and assert success plus creation of an audit warning record containing actor, round, timestamp, and override reason in persistent audit storage.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the review loop cannot finalize PASS unless a fresh review-agent round is executed on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is sequencing/state coupling (diff version, phase transitions, and round execution marker). This can be caught without real LLM nondeterminism; the minimum is verifying orchestration across modules and persisted state.
behaviors.test_description: Create a run where Phase 4 applies code fixes and diff/version advances. Do not execute a new review-agent round; attempt finalize PASS and assert blocked with reason 'fresh round required'. Then execute one new round on the updated diff (stubbed deterministic agent output is sufficient) and assert finalization is allowed only after that round record is present and linked to the new diff version.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings, the flow emits an escalation outcome instead of PASS.
behaviors.justification: This depends on interaction of round counter policy + persisted unresolved severity findings + final outcome emitter. Unit tests can miss policy/state retrieval mismatches that cause accidental PASS.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a run with max rounds reached and at least one unresolved MUST-FIX finding in storage. Execute the real outcome-resolution step. Assert outcome is ESCALATION (or equivalent), PASS is not emitted, and escalation payload includes unresolved finding identifiers/severity for follow-up.
